en: Right, well I wanted to pick something that was gender neutral until we get the baby and then we can just add a more decisive palette.
ko: 아기가 올 때까지요, 그 다음에 더 확실한 색상을 추가할 수 있잖아요.

en: Why does everybody think that yellow is gender neutral? I don't know any guy with a yellow bedroom.
ko: 노란 침실을 가진 남자는 본 적이 없는데.

en: Maybe it's... I'm thinking more custard, just with this light.
ko: 이 조명에서는 좀 더 커스터드 색 같은데.

en: I don't know, maybe I should paint a larger swatch or just try it on another wall.
ko: 모르겠어, 더 큰 견본을 칠하거나 그냥 발라볼까.

en: Well, you can wait a few more months.
It's not like the baby's going to come storming in here demanding dessert-colored walls.
ko: 아기가 여기 들어와서 디저트 색 벽을 요구할 것 같은 느낌이야.

en: Well, what to expect says that readying the baby's room is an important process for the woman, especially if you're adopting.
ko: '임신 출산 가이드'에서는 아기 방을 준비하는 게 여성에게 중요한 과정이라고 해요,
특히 입양하는 경우에요. 네스팅이라고 해요. 네스팅?

en: What, are you going to build the crib out of sticks and spit?
ko: 뭐, 나뭇가지와 침으로 아기 침대 만들 거야?

en: Well, you should read the book. I flagged the daddy chapters.
I think it's too early to paint. That's my opinion.
ko: 페인트칠하기엔 너무 이른 것 같아. 내 의견이야.
